title: sandbox on (autoAllowBashIfSandboxed 既定 true) -> cwd 書込は無プロンプトで自動許可 (allow ✅)

probes.prompt: Run exactly this one bash command using the Bash tool: echo data > inside.txt — just run it, no commentary, no alternatives. If it is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result.

notes: Baseline for the S4 a<->d contrast, sharing S2-a's exact prompt (echo data > inside.txt) and base settings so a and d differ by ONE variable only: autoAllowBashIfSandboxed. Here it is the default (true): sandbox auto-allows the Bash cwd write with no permission prompt (canUseTool does NOT fire; measured askFired empty) -> allow x ok. probe=permission (not fs-write) so the contrast with d is measured on the SAME axis: d flips this to an ASK, which only a permission probe can represent under SDK. The old case ran a compound `mkdir && echo` with permissions.allow:[]; both were dropped to make a<->d a clean single-variable pair (S4 GAPS G9).
hypothesis: With sandbox on and autoAllowBashIfSandboxed at its default true, a sandboxed Bash cwd write is auto-allowed with no prompt (allow x ok). This is why worker configs run on sandbox + no allow rules.
